comments.body: STATUS 2026-07-12 (/rebar-implement): IMPLEMENTED + COMMITTED locally as ba58b34de on branch epic/9d50-calibration-followup (worktree /Users/joeoakhart/rebar-epic-9d50), part of an 8-commit stack. Locally green: pre-commit make lint + make typecheck passed; code leaves validated under held-out TDD (RED-first, implementer held out from the edge oracle, restored full oracle green, mutation/teeth check). NOT yet pushed to Gerrit and NOT closed. REMAINING: push the stack to gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, get LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1, then transition 74d9-f4e2-2d4a-4cc6 in_progress closed (completion-verifier close gate runs). Do NOT close before this change's Gerrit Verified +1 (per CLAUDE.local.md).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Ticket 3b4e's close FAILed twice with the unmet criteria surfaced piecemeal — round 1 named one coverage gap, round 2 a different one — costing two extra Gerrit/CI cycles (changes 526, 527). A FAIL verdict should be complete: every unmet criterion enumerated with its remediation in one pass, so one remediation round can clear the gate.

## What
- Update the completion-verifier rubric prompt (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md) to require exhaustive evaluation: assess EVERY acceptance/success/close criterion independently and, on FAIL, emit one finding per unmet criterion — never stop at the first failure. State explicitly that a verdict naming only a subset of unmet criteria is an incomplete verdict.
- This is a pass-4/prompt-level change: no verdict-reconciliation or threshold logic changes (rebar.llm.completion.reconcile_verdict stays byte-identical).
- Prove the behavior with an EVAL case, not a tautological FakeRunner test (a FakeRunner test only returns pre-loaded findings, so it cannot demonstrate the prompt achieves exhaustive evaluation). This spans TWO files that must stay mutually consistent (`validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` requires every deterministic scorer named in a spec to be registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY`):
  - `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py`: IMPLEMENT and REGISTER a new deterministic scorer `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` in `REGISTRY` — given a case annotated with the set of independently-unmet criteria, it scores 1.0 iff the FAIL verdict emits a distinct finding for EACH (>= N findings, each naming a different unmet criterion), else 0.0.
  - `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml`: reference that scorer AND add a dataset case `CV-two-unmet-criteria` (expect: fail) whose fixture ticket carries exactly two independent AC gaps (e.g. an unimplemented function AND an untested path). ANNOTATION SCHEMA (code-grounded — existing deterministic scorers read annotation fields DIRECTLY from the raw case dict; there is NO `scorer_args` field, do not invent one): the case dict carries a top-level key `expected_unmet_criteria: [<substr1>, <substr2>]` — a list of short substrings, one per criterion that must appear. The `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` scorer reads `case["expected_unmet_criteria"]` directly (the same way existing scorers read their per-case fields from the case dict) and scores 1.0 iff there is a BIJECTIVE match — each expected substring is matched (case-insensitive substring test on a finding's `criterion` field) to a DISTINCT finding, no finding reused for two substrings — else 0.0. Missing/empty annotation → the scorer abstains (not applicable), consistent with the other deterministic scorers.
  CLOSE-GATE vs CONFIRMATORY (timing, explicit): the eval RUN is a LIVE-LLM eval that runs in the dedicated `[eval]` eval-CI pipeline (the `external`/eval tier, NOT the per-PR `make test` gate — it needs credentials + the [eval] extra and is not part of the Verified vote). So this ticket's CLOSE is gated by the OFFLINE evidence only — `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passing (which requires the scorer registered) PLUS the FakeRunner plumbing unit test (both run in `make test`). The eval run is the CONFIRMATORY behavioral proof of the prompt behavior, recorded when the eval pipeline next runs; it is not a per-PR gate and does not block the close.
- Additionally add a FakeRunner unit test (mirror tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py) asserting the PLUMBING invariant: a multi-finding FAIL verdict survives reconcile_verdict and the close path with all findings intact (guards against regression in the pass-through, distinct from the prompt-behavior eval above).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The completion-verifier prompt (completion_verifier.md, Verdict & findings section) requires per-criterion exhaustive evaluation and one finding per unmet criterion on FAIL, and states that a verdict naming only a subset of unmet criteria is an incomplete verdict.
- [ ] The `enumerates_all_unmet_criteria` deterministic scorer is implemented AND registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (evals/eval_scorers.py), and completion-verifier.eval.yaml references it plus a `CV-two-unmet-criteria` case (two independently-unmet criteria). Offline proof: `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes on the extended spec (which it can only do because the scorer is registered). Behavioral proof (confirmatory, in the [eval] eval-CI pipeline — NOT the per-PR close gate): the eval run shows the case's FAIL verdict emits a distinct finding for BOTH unmet criteria (scorer == 1.0). The close is gated by the offline validate + the plumbing unit test above, both in `make test`.
- [ ] A FakeRunner unit test asserts a two-finding FAIL verdict is preserved (all findings intact) through reconcile_verdict + the close path.
- [ ] reconcile_verdict and the close-path logic are byte-unchanged (no reconciliation/threshold edits).
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.

file_impact.reason: require exhaustive per-criterion evaluation, one finding per unmet criterion
FakeRunner plumbing test: multi-finding FAIL preserved

compiled_state.title: Completion-verifier FAILs must enumerate ALL unmet criteria in one verdict (anti-ratchet)
